standard_description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.
subject: Math

Problem 1
Do taller people tend to have bigger hands?
Option 1: Collect data measurements.
Students measure their hand spans in centimeters and heights in inches.
Record the values in a table.

Use the hypothetical data set provided
here

b.   Based on the graph, how would you answer the question about whether taller people tend to have bigger hands? Explain your reasoning.

khan_exercises.title: Positive and negative linear associations from scatter plots
khan_exercises.description: Practice identifying the types of associations shown in scatter plots. Sometimes we see linear associations (positive or negative), sometimes we see non-linear associations (the data seems to follow a curve), and other times we don't see any association at all.
topic_path: Observing more patterns in scatter plots
Positive and negative linear associations from scatter plots

items.question_content:
The plot shown below describes the relationship between the average income in a city and the average rent for a $1$-bedroom apartment in that city.

![A graph plots Average rent for a 1 bedroom, in dollars per month, from 0 to 2000, in increments of 100, on the y-axis, versus Average income, in dollars per year, from 30000 to 50000, in increments of 5000, on the x-axis. 17 points rise diagonally in a loose cluster between (36500, 825) and (58500, 1800). All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45ae96959e3f04f19f5a24ccfbd0f9ed4072c307)

**What is the best description of this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Can you picture a line going through the data?

Yes. Here's what it might look like:

![A graph plots Average rent for a 1 bedroom, in dollars per month, from 0 to 2000, in increments of 100, on the y-axis, versus Average income, in dollars per year, from 30000 to 50000, in increments of 5000, on the x-axis. 17 points rise diagonally in a loose cluster between (36500, 825) and (58500, 1800). A regression line rises diagonally through the center of the cluster from (33000, 0) to (60000, 1650). All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cade994f5d32781aad79f99b00824215f4dcc97a)
The line we drew through the data has a positive slope, so this scatter plot shows a positive linear association.

The answer:

Positive linear association

items.question_content:
The graph shown below shows the relationship between the age of drivers and the number of car accidents per $100$ drivers in the year $2009$.

![A graph plots Accidents per 100 drivers, from 9 to 24, in increments of 1, on the y-axis, versus Age, in years, from 16 to 25, in increments of 0.5, on the x-axis. 9 points fall diagonally in a loose, but narrow cluster between (16, 23) and (24, 11). All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ffa167e6740a1093649c055b2edff49e66268c7d)

**What is the best description of this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Can you picture a line going through the data?

Yes. Here's what it might look like:

![A graph plots Accidents per 100 drivers, from 9 to 24, in increments of 1, on the y-axis, versus Age, in years, from 16 to 25, in increments of 0.5, on the x-axis. 9 points fall diagonally in a loose, but narrow cluster between (16, 23) and (24, 11). A regression line falls through the center of the points from (0, 22) to (25, 10.9). All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f31665c8b6de7bcf29a2ecfb0880b13dda2d5bb5)
The line we drew through the data has a negative slope, so this scatter plot shows a negative linear association.

The answer:

Negative linear association

items.question_content:
Vincent gathered groups of $1$ to $8$ people and gave every group the same puzzle to solve. He recorded the number of minutes it took each group to solve the puzzle and plotted the results. 

![A graph plots Time, in minutes, from 0 to 20, in increments of 5, on the y-axis, versus Group size, from 0 to 8, in increments of 1, on the x-axis. Approximately 2 dozen points fall in a narrow curved pattern, with decreasing steepness, between (1, 20) and (8, 6.5). All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0cddf907c3c21c1df2a78c56b77c3e61c54612d3)

**What is the best description of this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Can you picture a line going through the data?

Any line we might draw wouldn't fit the data very well.

![A graph plots Time, in minutes, from 0 to 20, in increments of 5, on the y-axis, versus Group size, from 0 to 8, in increments of 1, on the x-axis. Approximately 2 dozen points fall in a narrow curved pattern, with decreasing steepness, between (1, 20) and (8, 6.5). A regression line falls from (0, 14) to (10, 2.5). All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bc810cf57ec601951c43222c2d9f9747e3222014)
Might a curve fit the data better than a line?

Yes. Here's what it might look like:

![A graph plots Time, in minutes, from 0 to 20, in increments of 5, on the y-axis, versus Group size, from 0 to 8, in increments of 1, on the x-axis. Approximately 2 dozen points fall in a narrow curved pattern, with decreasing steepness, between (1, 20) and (8, 6.5). A regression curve falls with decreasing steepness through (1, 17), (2, 10), and (7.5, 6). All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7e58b22f47e219ed58ff0ae8ce959090a0f9bf29)

The answer:

Nonlinear association

items.question_content:
Aubree wanted to see if there is a connection between the time a given exam takes place and the average score of this exam.  She collected data about all the exams from the previous year and arranged the average scores of the exams according to the period they took place, as shown below. 

![A graph plots Average score, from 0 to 100, in increments of 5, on the y-axis, versus Period, from 0 to 9, in increments of 0.5, on the x-axis. Dozens of points extend nearly horizontally rightward, in a loose, wide cluster with points plotted between x = 1 and x = 8, and between y = 39 and y = 78.  All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bfe7587ee8b8848663a9cf236ab5bb0a174a9c06)

**What is the best description of this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Can you picture a line going through the data?
No. Any line we might draw wouldn't fit the data very well.
It would also be impossible to draw a curve that fit the data very well.

The answer:

No association
